source_intake_record.raw_question_text: An object is placed 10 cm in front of a convering lens of focal length 10 cm . A diverging lens of focal length -15 cm is placed 5 cm behind the converging lens (Fig. 1.27). Find the size of the final image.
extraction_notes: image entries are PIL object string representations rather than explicit file paths or URLs

issues: metadata.image_paths contains PIL object string representations rather than explicit file paths or URLs

image_dependency: Question explicitly references `Fig. 1.27`, indicating an associated diagram is part of the original presentation
However, the text itself states the lens focal lengths, spacing, and object distance, so the core setup is largely recoverable without inspecting the image
multi_step: Solving requires sequential optics reasoning across two lenses rather than a single direct lookup
The second lens depends on the intermediate image formed by the first lens, so at least two linked calculations are needed
The requested output is final image size, which typically requires combining image-position reasoning with magnification
verifiability: Answer is a short textual numeric ratio (`1.5 times`), which is straightforward to compare against derived results
`registry_passed` is true, and both question and answer are present in the registry manifests
This is a standard physics problem with a determinate setup, so the result can be checked by rule-based or symbolic calculation
